title.zh: 在鼓钟之山宴饮百神的神人
title.en: The Divine Personage Who Feasted the Hundred Gods

summary.zh: 只在中次七经留下遗迹的神人：休与山有他祷神的五色棋石，鼓钟山是他宴饮百神之处，高前山寒泉号称帝台之浆。
summary.en: A divine personage known only through relics in the seventh central sequence: five-colored gaming stones on Mount Xiuyu, a feasting-ground for the hundred gods on Mount Guzhong, and a cold spring called his draught.

description.zh:
帝台没有形貌描写，郭璞只注了一句“神人名”。他在《中次七经》里留下三处遗迹。苦山山系的首山休与之山上有“帝台之棋”——五色而有花纹的石子，形状像鹌鹑蛋；经文说“帝台之石，所以祷百神者也，服之不蛊”，那是他祷祀百神用的石子，佩在身上可以不受蛊毒。

鼓钟之山，“帝台之所以觞百神也”——他在这座山上设酒宴请百神。高前之山上有一眼泉水，“甚寒而清，帝台之浆也，饮之者不心痛”。棋、觞、浆，三样遗物拼出一个从未露面的身影：一位在嵩山左近的群山之间祷神、宴神的主人，位分足以把“百神”尽数请到席上。
description.en:
Ditai has no described form; Guo Pu's note says only 'the name of a divine personage.' He survives in the seventh central sequence as three relics. On Mount Xiuyu, first peak of the Ku range, lie the 'gaming stones of Ditai': five-colored, patterned pebbles shaped like quail eggs. With these stones, the text says, he prayed to the hundred gods, and whoever wears one is proof against gu poison.

Mount Guzhong, 'Drum-and-Bell Mountain,' is 'where Ditai feasted the hundred gods with wine.' And on Mount Gaoqian rises a spring, piercingly cold and clear, called 'the draught of Ditai'; those who drink of it are freed from pain of the heart. Stones, banquet-ground and draught sketch a figure never once shown: a host among the hills around Mount Song who prayed to the gods and poured them wine, of rank enough to seat all hundred of them at his table.

value.zh: 休与之山帝台之棋，五色文石如鹑卵
value.en: Gaming stones on Mount Xiuyu, five-colored, shaped like quail eggs
Mount Guzhong, where he feasted the hundred gods

value.zh: 高前之山帝台之浆，饮之不心痛
value.en: The cold spring of Mount Gaoqian, his draught against pain of the heart

sources.en: Guo Pu, Commentary on the Shanhaijing
